issues.description: Double-bit ECC error in GPU memory.
issues.typical_resolution: Drain the node, run DCGM diagnostics, and replace the affected GPU if diagnostics fail or the error recurs.
issues.confidence_notes: A DBE is strongly correlated with hardware failure when temperatures are otherwise normal.

issues.description: Graphics engine exception that often maps to a driver or CUDA stack mismatch.
issues.typical_resolution: Restart the GPU driver stack, review CUDA and driver compatibility, and re-run the workload with debug logging enabled.
issues.confidence_notes: Context from running jobs is important because some workloads can trigger recoverable graphics engine faults.

issues.description: GPU has fallen off the bus, indicating a severe connectivity or hardware event.
issues.typical_resolution: Cordon and drain the node, power cycle if needed, inspect PCIe health, and escalate to hardware support if the fault persists.
issues.confidence_notes: Confidence increases when the node is partially unreachable or when multiple GPUs on the same host show degraded telemetry.

issues.description: ECC page retirement or row remapping event that is usually informational until it becomes recurrent.
issues.typical_resolution: Monitor the node, track recurrence, and escalate if page retirement frequency or retired-page counts trend upward.
issues.confidence_notes: Single events are often benign, but recurrence over a short window can indicate degradation.

issues.description: GPU utilization dropped sharply, which can be either a workload issue or normal job behavior.
issues.typical_resolution: Inspect the active jobs and verify whether the workload is expected to be CPU-bound or waiting on input stages before taking infrastructure action.
issues.confidence_notes: Running-job context is required because preprocessing and checkpoint phases can produce normal low-utilization windows.

issues.typical_resolution: Check cooling, airflow, and fan health, and consider workload throttling if temperatures exceed safe limits.
issues.confidence_notes: Thermal confidence is higher when repeated thermal violations align with ambient or fan anomalies.

issues.description: Container or process was terminated by the out-of-memory killer.
issues.typical_resolution: Recommend reducing batch size or increasing requested memory and document the user pattern for future triage.
issues.confidence_notes: OOM kill is generally attributable to workload sizing rather than node failure when host telemetry is healthy.

issues.description: Distributed training timed out during collective communication.
issues.typical_resolution: Check interconnect health, NCCL logs, and GPU status across participants before deciding whether the issue is workload or infrastructure driven.
issues.confidence_notes: Systemic suspicion is warranted if several nodes or jobs report similar communication failures within a short interval.

issues.description: One or more node-critical services such as exporters or health agents have failed.
issues.typical_resolution: Restart the failed service, verify it stays active, and escalate only if the service repeatedly fails or masks a deeper node issue.
issues.confidence_notes: Confidence increases when the alert is limited to one or more systemd services and node telemetry is otherwise healthy.

issues.description: A PCIe bus path to the GPU subsystem is inaccessible.
issues.typical_resolution: Quarantine the node, terminate or drain workloads according to policy, and escalate to hardware operations for deeper inspection.
issues.confidence_notes: This alert is highly correlated with a severe host or device path failure, especially when it fires repeatedly.

issues.description: PCIe link width or speed does not match the expected negotiated state.
keywords: pcie link width and current speed mismatch
issues.typical_resolution: Treat as a hardware degradation signal, correlate with related PCIe alerts, and schedule maintenance or terminate the node if combined with accessibility failures.
issues.confidence_notes: Confidence rises when the mismatch co-occurs with bus accessibility failures or repeated fire counts.
